seoTitle: Songle Relay Products | Power, Signal, Automotive, SSR Distributor
seoDescription: Browse Songle relay products including power relays, signal relays, automotive relays, and solid state relays. Authorized distributor with technical support.

faqs.question: What is the difference between power relays and signal relays?
faqs.answer: Power relays are designed for high-current switching applications, typically handling 5A to 30A for industrial and appliance control. Signal relays are designed for low-current applications, typically handling less than 2A for telecommunications and control circuits. Power relays have larger contacts and higher contact ratings, while signal relays offer faster switching and higher sensitivity.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use power relays for high-current industrial/appliance applications. Use signal relays for low-current telecom/control circuits.

faqs.question: How do I select the right relay contact rating?
faqs.answer: Select relay contact rating based on your load current with appropriate safety margin. For resistive loads, size relay at 125% of load current. For inductive loads (motors, solenoids), size at 150-200% of load current due to high inrush currents. Always verify voltage rating matches or exceeds your circuit voltage.
faqs.decisionGuide: Calculate load current and add safety margin. Contact our FAE for inductive load applications.

faqs.question: What is the difference between electromechanical and solid state relays?
faqs.answer: Electromechanical relays use mechanical contacts to switch circuits, offering low contact resistance and galvanic isolation. Solid state relays use semiconductor devices (thyristors, triacs) for switching, offering longer life, silent operation, and faster switching. SSRs have no moving parts but generate heat and may have higher on-state voltage drop.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use electromechanical relays for cost-sensitive applications. Use SSRs for high-speed switching or long-life requirements.

faqs.question: What coil voltage options are available for Songle relays?
faqs.answer: Songle relays offer various coil voltage options including 5V, 12V, 24V DC and 120V, 240V AC. Standard industrial relays typically use 24V DC coils. Appliance relays commonly use 12V DC. Automotive relays typically use 12V or 24V DC. Select coil voltage that matches your control circuit voltage.
faqs.decisionGuide: Match relay coil voltage to your control circuit. 24V DC is standard for industrial applications.

faqs.question: What contact configurations are available?
faqs.answer: Songle relays offer various contact configurations: SPST (Single Pole Single Throw) for simple on/off switching, SPDT (Single Pole Double Throw) for switching between two circuits, DPST (Double Pole Single Throw) for simultaneous switching of two circuits, and DPDT (Double Pole Double Throw) for versatile switching applications. Select based on your circuit switching requirements.
faqs.decisionGuide: Select contact configuration based on number of circuits to switch and switching logic required.

categories.name: Power Relays
categories.description: High-capacity electromechanical relays designed for industrial and appliance applications. Power relays from Songle offer reliable switching for currents up to 30A with various contact configurations.
categories.longDescription: Songle power relays provide reliable switching solutions for industrial control and home appliance applications. As an authorized Songle distributor, BeiLuo offers power relays with contact ratings from 10A to 30A, supporting both AC and DC loads. These relays feature robust contact systems, high dielectric strength, and long electrical life. Available in PCB mount, panel mount, and socket configurations to suit various installation requirements. All power relays carry UL, VDE, and CQC certifications for global market compliance. Our selection guide helps you choose the right relay based on contact rating, coil voltage, and mounting type.

selectionGuide.title: How to Select Power Relays
selectionGuide.description: Comprehensive guide for selecting power relays based on contact rating, coil voltage, and application requirements.

faqs.question: What contact rating do I need for my application?
faqs.answer: Select contact rating based on load current with safety margin. For resistive loads (heaters, lamps), use 125% of rated current. For inductive loads (motors, transformers), use 150-200% due to inrush currents. Songle power relays range from 10A to 30A contact ratings.
faqs.decisionGuide: Calculate load current and add appropriate safety margin. Contact FAE for inductive load applications.

faqs.question: What is the difference between SRA and SRD series?
faqs.answer: SRA series offers 20A contact rating in compact T74 package, ideal for appliance applications. SRD series offers 10A rating in T73 package, suitable for lower current industrial control. Both offer PCB and socket mounting options with various coil voltages.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use SRA for 15-20A appliance applications. Use SRD for 5-10A control applications.

faqs.question: Can power relays switch DC loads?
faqs.answer: Yes, but DC switching capacity is typically lower than AC due to arc extinguishing challenges. Songle power relays specify both AC and DC ratings. For DC loads, verify the DC switching rating in the datasheet. DC inductive loads require special consideration due to high arc energy.
faqs.decisionGuide: Verify DC switching rating in datasheet. Use arc suppression for DC inductive loads.

faqs.question: What is the electrical life expectancy?
faqs.answer: Songle power relays typically offer 100,000 operations at rated load. Life expectancy increases at lighter loads - up to 1 million operations at minimum load. Life decreases with inductive loads due to contact arcing. Proper arc suppression can extend relay life significantly.
faqs.decisionGuide: Size relay appropriately and consider arc suppression for maximum relay life.

faqs.question: What mounting options are available?
faqs.answer: Songle power relays offer PCB mounting for direct board installation, socket mounting for easy replacement, and panel mounting for enclosure installation. PCB mount is most common for appliance applications. Socket mount is preferred for industrial maintenance access.
faqs.decisionGuide: Select PCB mount for appliances. Select socket mount for industrial maintenance access.

products.name: SRA 24V DC 20A Power Relay
products.shortDescription: 20A power relay with 24V DC coil, SPDT contacts, PCB mount for industrial and appliance applications.
descriptionParagraphs: The SRA-24VDC-CL is a high-capacity power relay designed for industrial control and appliance applications.
With 20A contact rating and 24V DC coil, this relay provides reliable switching for motors, heaters, and power distribution.
The SPDT contact configuration offers versatile switching capability for various circuit designs.

faeReview.author: Michael Chen
faeReview.title: Senior FAE - Industrial Control
faeReview.content: The SRA series has been my go-to recommendation for appliance and industrial control applications. The 20A rating handles most residential and light commercial loads effectively. I particularly appreciate the low coil power consumption - at 0.36W, it generates minimal heat and can be driven directly by most microcontroller outputs. The compact T74 package fits well in space-constrained designs. In my experience, the contact life is excellent when used within ratings - I've seen these relays operate for years in washing machines and HVAC equipment. For inductive loads like motors, I always recommend adding arc suppression to maximize contact life. The UL and VDE certifications make this suitable for global appliance applications.
faeReview.highlight: Reliable 20A switching with low coil power for industrial and appliance applications

alternativeParts.reason: Lower coil voltage for 12V control systems
Higher current capacity for heavy loads

companionParts.description: 12V coil version for lower voltage control
PCB socket for easy relay replacement
SPST version for simple on/off control

faqs.question: What is the maximum switching voltage for this relay?
faqs.answer: The SRA-24VDC-CL is rated for 240V AC or 30V DC maximum switching voltage. For AC applications, this covers standard residential and commercial voltages (120V/240V). For DC applications, the 30V rating is suitable for 24V DC control systems and automotive applications.
faqs.decisionGuide: Verify your circuit voltage is within 240V AC or 30V DC limits.

faqs.question: Can this relay be used for motor control?
faqs.answer: Yes, the 20A rating is suitable for motors up to approximately 1.5HP at 120V or 3HP at 240V. However, motors are inductive loads with high inrush currents (5-7x running current). For motor applications, size the relay at 150-200% of motor FLA and use arc suppression across contacts.
faqs.decisionGuide: Size at 150-200% of motor FLA. Use arc suppression for motor loads.

faqs.question: What is the coil current consumption?
faqs.answer: The coil consumes approximately 15mA at 24V DC (0.36W). This low power consumption allows direct drive from microcontroller outputs or logic circuits. The coil has built-in suppression diode for DC coils to protect drive circuits from back-EMF.
faqs.decisionGuide: Verify your drive circuit can supply 15mA at 24V DC.

faqs.question: What is the contact resistance?
faqs.answer: Initial contact resistance is typically less than 100 milliohms. Contact resistance may increase over life due to contact wear and oxidation. High contact resistance causes voltage drop and heating. Replace relay if contact resistance exceeds 200 milliohms or if heating is observed.
faqs.decisionGuide: Measure contact resistance if experiencing voltage drop or heating issues.

faqs.question: Is this relay suitable for outdoor use?
faqs.answer: The standard SRA relay is rated for -40°C to +85°C operating temperature, suitable for many outdoor applications. However, the relay is not sealed against moisture or dust. For harsh outdoor environments, consider using a protective enclosure or selecting a sealed relay variant.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use protective enclosure for outdoor applications. Verify temperature range for your environment.

products.name: SRD 12V DC 10A Power Relay
products.shortDescription: 10A power relay with 12V DC coil, SPDT contacts, compact T73 package for control applications.
descriptionParagraphs: The SRD-12VDC-SL-C is a compact power relay ideal for industrial control and appliance applications.
With 10A contact rating and 12V DC coil, this relay is perfect for microcontroller-based control systems.
The compact T73 package saves PCB space while providing reliable switching performance.

faeReview.author: Sarah Liu
faeReview.title: FAE - Control Systems
faeReview.content: The SRD series is my standard recommendation for microcontroller-based control applications. The 12V coil is ideal for automotive and battery-powered systems. At only 0.36W coil power, it can be driven directly by MCU outputs with a simple transistor driver. The compact T73 package is perfect for space-constrained designs - I've used these in smart home devices where board space is limited. The 10A rating handles most control applications while the SPDT configuration provides flexibility for circuit design. Quality has been consistent across batches, which is important for production designs. For high-volume appliance applications, I recommend the SRA series for higher current capacity.
faeReview.highlight: Compact 10A relay perfect for microcontroller control systems

alternativeParts.reason: Higher current capacity with same coil voltage

faqs.question: What is the difference between SRD and SRA series?
faqs.answer: SRD series offers 10A rating in compact T73 package, ideal for control applications. SRA series offers 20A rating in larger T74 package for higher power applications. Both offer similar coil options and SPDT configuration. SRD is more compact and cost-effective for lower current needs.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use SRD for 5-10A control. Use SRA for 15-20A power applications.

faqs.question: Can this relay be driven directly by Arduino or Raspberry Pi?
faqs.answer: The 12V coil requires a driver circuit - cannot be driven directly by 3.3V or 5V logic. Use a transistor (2N2222, BC337) or MOSFET driver. Coil current is 30mA at 12V. For direct logic drive, consider the SRD-5VDC version with appropriate driver.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use transistor driver for 12V coil. Consider 5V version for simpler drive.

faqs.question: What is the operate and release time?
faqs.answer: Typical operate time (pull-in) is 10ms maximum. Release time (drop-out) is 5ms maximum. These times are measured at rated coil voltage and room temperature. Actual times may vary with coil voltage and temperature.
faqs.decisionGuide: Verify switching speed meets your application timing requirements.

faqs.question: What is the insulation resistance?
faqs.answer: Minimum insulation resistance is 1000 Mohms between coil and contacts, and between contacts. This is measured at 500V DC megger test. High insulation resistance ensures safety and prevents leakage currents between circuits.
faqs.decisionGuide: Verify insulation rating meets your safety requirements.

faqs.question: Is this relay RoHS compliant?
faqs.answer: Yes, all Songle SRD series relays are RoHS compliant (Restriction of Hazardous Substances). They contain no lead, mercury, cadmium, hexavalent chromium, PBB, or PBDE above permitted levels. Compliance certificates are available upon request.
faqs.decisionGuide: Suitable for RoHS-compliant products. Contact us for compliance certificates.
